ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest19237: <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>- </grad></shadow><grad from=#42E3D9 to=#B0EBE7 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>FTB Essentials</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> cooldowns increase with the difficulty.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>- </grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#49FF38 to=#9FFF96 f=0.8>Parrying</grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> becomes harder with the difficulty.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>This pack includes a </grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#DB762E to=#D9A37E f=0.8>unique progression system</grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>, thus I suggest checking out Main Progression questline.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>This is a </grad></shadow><grad from=#4DDB58 to=#C1F5C5 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Terraria-inspired</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>, </grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFC438 to=#FFE4A3 f=0.8>content-packed</grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> modpack built to give </grad></shadow><grad from=#4DDB58 to=#C1F5C5 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Minecraft</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> a whole new style of progression and adventure.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>The </grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8>quest book</grad></shadow><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> is your main guide. It will walk you through the features, mechanics, and progression paths so you don't get lost around.</grad></shadow>
## <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.6>Things you need to know</grad></shadow>

&l<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8>Boss-locked progression:</grad></shadow>&r <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>You can't just craft everything right away. Defeating bosses unlocks A LOT of new stuff.</grad></shadow>

&l<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8>Unique Difficulty System:</grad></shadow>&r <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>The difficulty you choose at the start of the game will change a lot.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=CF8019 to=CF8019>We all start from somewhere.</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest223AC: <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Reforging your items is a straightforward way to get a lot stronger. It can provide nearly any buff in the game to your equipment.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>- A </grad><grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8>tier material</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> from the list below.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6><shadow a=0.3>Material List:</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#30FF68 to=#B6FCC9 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Tier 1 / Common - Limestone Chunk</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Tier 2 / Uncommon - Pyrite</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Tier 3 / Rare - Galena</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#6E2BFF to=#CCB5FF f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Tier 4 / Epic - Charoite</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#FA732A to=#FFCFB5 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Tier 5 / Legendary - Crown Topaz</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#910308 to=#9C6466 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Tier 6 / Mythic - Painite</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6><shadow a=0.3>4. Perfect Rolls</shadow></grad>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>There is a 1.0%% chance to trigger a </grad><grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8>Perfect</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> reforge. A perfect roll removes every single downside from the modifiers and adds a badass custom item border.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Here is a quick guide on how to use it:</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6><shadow a=0.3>5. Cleansing Stones</shadow></grad>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>If you end up with modifiers you don't want, you can use a </grad><grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8>Cleansing Stone</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> to completely wipe the current reforge stats and try again.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#606060>Thanks to </grad><wave a=0.3 f=0.4 w=0.4><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8>@d0ugdimad0me</grad></wave><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#606060> in our Discord server for creating this custom system just for Linggango.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6><shadow a=0.3>1. Craft an Anvil</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6><shadow a=0.3>2. Open the Reforge Tab</shadow></grad>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Right-click the anvil and select the Reforge Gear tab.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6><shadow a=0.3>3. Insert the Required Items</shadow></grad>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>The system requires three inputs:</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>- The gear you want to reforge (</grad><grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8>swords, bows, and armor</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>).</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>- The item's primary repair material (if nothing else works, use </grad><grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8>iron</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>).</grad></shadow>
{image:customtextures:textures/anvilcrafting.png width:100 height:50 align:center}
{image:customtextures:textures/reforgingtab.png width:150 height:150 align:center}
<typewriter><grad from=#A834CF to=#A834CF>I love Apotheo-... Oh, it's not it.</grad>
So cool
<grad from=#FF2B2B to=#FF2B2B>Combat:</grad><grad from=#D042FF to=#D042FF> Reforging System</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest3010F: ### <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Alliances System</shadow></grad>

<grad from=#CC0000 to=#D64242 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>- false:</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> Attacks and harmful potion effects are completely blocked between allies.</grad></shadow>
### <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Resource Requests</shadow></grad>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Allies can request resources directly from each other using these commands:</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally request item</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Asks for the item your ally is holding in their main hand.</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally request xp</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Requests a specific number of </grad></shadow><grad from=#00D411 to=#36CF43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>XP</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> levels.</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally request accept</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Accepts the pending item or </grad></shadow><grad from=#00D411 to=#36CF43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>XP</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> request.</grad></shadow>
### <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Additional Info</shadow></grad>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#606060>- All members share a unified alliance group once joined.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#606060>- Friendly fire rules apply to both direct physical attacks and harmful potion effects.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#606060>- Requests are stored until accepted. If the requester logs off, the pending request is automatically removed.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Alliances let players form groups to cooperate, share resources, and avoid accidental PvP. Once you are part of an alliance, you can invite others, manage friendly fire, and request items or </grad></shadow><grad from=#00D411 to=#36CF43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>XP</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> directly from your allies.</grad></shadow>
### <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Forming Alliances</shadow></grad>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally invite</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Sends an invite. The recipient will see a clickable </grad></shadow><grad from=#00D411 to=#36CF43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>[ACCEPT]</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> button in chat.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally accept</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Accepts a pending invite and joins the alliance.</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally disband</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Disbands the alliance, removes all members, and notifies everyone.</grad></shadow>
### <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Friendly Fire</shadow></grad>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/ally rules friendlyfire <true/false></shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Toggles whether allies can damage each other.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00D411 to=#36CF43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>- true:</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> Allies can hurt each other.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=#CCAC74 to=#CCAC74>Don't kill your friends, please.</grad>
useful
<grad from=#FF91F0 to=#FF91F0>Multiplayer:</grad><grad from=#FFD791 to=#FFD791> Allience</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest31EEC: <grad from=#787878 to=#787878><shadow a=0.3>Explorer's Compass</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> allows you to </grad></shadow><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>locate</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> any structure in the </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>world</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>. Instead of pointing toward </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>spawn</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>, you can select a specific </grad></shadow><grad from=#787878 to=#787878><shadow a=0.3>structure type</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> from its interface, and the needle will guide you directly to the nearest one.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=#5E5E5E to=#5E5E5E>You'll have it in your inventory 24/7.</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest380F3: <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Dodging System and Movement</shadow></grad>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>The mechanic has a built-in </grad></shadow><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>cooldown</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> so it cannot be spammed.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Dodging is performed by pressing </grad></shadow><grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>R</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>When activated, it grants a brief window of </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>invincibility frames</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>, allowing you to completely avoid incoming </grad></shadow><grad from=#CC0000 to=#D64242 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>damage</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>. It can also be used mid-air, making it a great utility tool for quick repositioning or maneuvering through tough spots.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>The mechanic has a built-in </grad></shadow><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>cooldown</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> so it cannot be spammed. However, certain enchantments can reduce this </grad></shadow><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>cooldown</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> or enhance the dodge itself, turning it into a much more useful thing in the future.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>-➤ Swipe to next page for parrying.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Parrying is performed by pressing </grad></shadow><grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>F</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Reflects incoming attacks, keeping you </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>unharmed</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> while </grad></shadow><grad from=#CC0000 to=#D64242 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>dishing back damage</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> to your attacker.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>The more you parry in a row, the stronger it gets, eventually scaling up to %%HP damage. Just like dodging, there’s a </grad></shadow><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>cooldown</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>, so make sure to time it right.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=#227FC9 to=#227FC9>It's not just about hitting back.</grad>
I be rolling parrying movementing yay
<grad from=#FF2B2B to=#FF2B2B>Combat:</grad><grad from=#2BA0FF to=#2BA0FF> Dodging Attacks, Movement \& Parry</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest4C373: <grad from=#787878 to=#787878><shadow a=0.3>Explorer's Compass</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> functions similarly but tracks </grad></shadow><grad from=#787878 to=#787878><shadow a=0.3>structures</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> instead of </grad></shadow><grad from=#50FF1F to=#50FF1F><shadow a=0.3>biomes</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>. You can search for any </grad></shadow><grad from=#50FF1F to=#50FF1F><shadow a=0.3>biome</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> in the game through its menu, and it will display the </grad></shadow><grad from=#CC0000 to=#D64242 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>distance</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> and point you toward the closest </grad></shadow><grad from=#CC0000 to=#D64242 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>coordinates</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=#3DC217 to=#3DC217>To put it simple, it's a Biome Locator.</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest55A0C: <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Amulets provide </grad></shadow><grad from=#D126D1 to=#D18AD1 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>unique buffs</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> at completely no cost. You will receive them right at the start of the </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>world</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>, giving you a helpful boost for </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>early-game survival</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> and progression.</grad></shadow>

<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>While they are great for getting your footing early on, they are strictly designed to </grad></shadow><grad from=#6DE32D to=#BDDEAB f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>help</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> you get started. Do not expect them to carry you all the way into the </grad></shadow><grad from=#CC0000 to=#BF8080 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>late game</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=#C2A617 to=#C2A617>Please, don't rely on them.</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest74EAD: <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Here is a list of the most important default keybinds to help you navigate the modpack:</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>C</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Cast your selected spell.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>G</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Activate your class skill.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>N</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - FTB Ultimine (hold to mine multiple blocks at once).</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Z</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Zoom camera.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>R (hovering over an item)</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Shows the crafting recipe for that item.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>U (hovering over an item)</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Shows what recipes that item is used in.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>R</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Roll / Dodge.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>F</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Parry incoming attacks.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>M</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Open the world map.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#00960B to=#38BA43 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Shift + B</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Open your equipped backpack.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=CF8019 to=CF8019>Let's get over with the keyboard.</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest794D5: <grad from=#1FFFF0 to=#1FFFF0><shadow a=0.3>FTB Essentials</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> provides a variety of </grad></shadow><grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>utility commands</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> to streamline your gameplay and make survival less frustrating.</grad></shadow>
[gap=3]
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/heal</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Restores your health and food bars to full.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/fly</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Toggles creative-style flight.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/invsee</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Opens and allows you to edit another player's inventory.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/nickfor</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Changes another player's chat nickname.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/mute</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Mutes a player in chat.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/unmute</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Unmutes a player in chat.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/teleport_last</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Teleports you to a player's last known location.</grad></shadow>
## <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Teleport Commands</shadow></grad>
[gap=3]
<grad from=#BD0000 to=#BD2B2B f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Their cooldowns vary depending on the difficulty you have chosen.</shadow></grad>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/back</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Teleports you back to your previous location. Can be used multiple times to retrace your steps.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/spawn</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Teleports you to the world spawn point.</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/rtp</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Teleports you to a random, safe location in the world (automatically avoids oceans).</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/home [name]</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Teleports you to a set home.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/sethome</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Sets a home at your current location.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/delhome</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Deletes a specific home.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/listhomes [player]</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Lists all registered homes for a player.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/warp</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Teleports you to a server warp point.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/setwarp</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Creates a server warp point at your location.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/delwarp</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Deletes a specific warp point.</grad></shadow>
## <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Misc Commands</shadow></grad>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/listwarps</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Lists all available server warps.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/tpa</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Sends a request to teleport to another player.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/tpahere</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Requests another player to teleport to your location.</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/tpaccept [id]</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Accepts a pending teleport request (clickable buttons will also appear in chat).</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/tpadeny [id]</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Denies a pending teleport request.</grad></shadow>
[gap=3]
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/kickme</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Kicks you from the server (useful if the bed bug returns).</grad></shadow>

<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/trashcan</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Opens an inventory screen that permanently deletes any items placed inside upon closing.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/hat</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Equips the item in your main hand into your helmet slot.</grad></shadow>
<grad from=#006EDB to=#3F91E0 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>/nick</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> - Changes your display nickname in chat.</grad></shadow>
## <grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>Cheat Commands</shadow></grad>
<typewriter><grad from=18C7BB to=18C7BB>That's a bit.. too powerful?</grad>
Woaw!
<grad from=#1FFFF0 to=#1FFFF0>FTB Essentials</grad>
ftbquests.chapter.introducing.quest7A545: <shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Parties allow you to team up with friends in </grad></shadow><grad from=#FF91F0 to=#C470B9 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>multiplayer</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> to complete </grad></shadow><grad from=#FCE938 to=#FFF6A6 f=0.8><shadow a=0.3>quests</shadow></grad><shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7> together and share your progress. You can find the party management icon in the top corner of your inventory screen.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6>2. Create Your Party</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Click the button to start a new party group.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6>3. Set a Name and Description</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Enter a custom name and a short description for your party.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6>4. Invite Your Friends</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Use the player list to send invites to your friends on the server.</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FF9E1F to=#FF9E1F f=0.6>1. Open the Teams Menu</grad></shadow>
<shadow a=0.3><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7>Open your inventory and click on the Teams icon.</grad></shadow>
<typewriter><grad from=#C973BE to=#C973BE>Playing with friends makes the modpack much more fun!</grad>

ftbquests.chapter.introducing.title: <shadow a=0.4><grad from=#35FCF9 to=#B8FFFE>Introduction</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest24B9: A rare and fiery material needed for high-tier fire and combat magic.
Dropped from &cAncient Knights&r, which can be awakened by breaking armor piles found within structures.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest26A7: Iron's Spellbooks introduces a complete magic system focused on casting powerful spells, upgrading your gear, and exploring dangerous structures.
&eEssential materials and spell scrolls can be found in structures.&r
A guide book for the premier magic mod, featuring high-quality spell content and powerful magical abilities.
This questline itself is highly inspired by the Beyond The Horizons modpack.
&8Nearly all credit for this questline goes to them. Thanks to their team for making such a beautiful questline.&r
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest2865: Powerful catalytic items used to infuse your equipment with extra attributes.
One item can be upgraded up to 3 times on the &dArcane Anvil&r.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest346D: A sacred tome filled with holy spells and protective villager magic.
Building a reputation with the villagers, especially by freeing captives from the &cArchevoker&r, will ensure one falls into your hands eventually. Or just craft it.
A regular book with a little blessing makes a suitable channel for divine magic.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest357F: A rare textbook detailing the deep secrets of high-tier water magic.
Return &9'The Sunken Fate'&r lore book to the &bAqua Grandmaster&r to obtain this guide.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest3B66: An essential apparatus for refining your magical fluids and reagents.
Allows you to salvage resources by dissolving scrolls back into ink, or combine lesser ingredients to improve the quality of your ink.
Used for working with ink and potions.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest3B8E: Powerful magical implements that can be wielded alongside your main weapon.
Can be equipped in the left hand.
When equipped, pressing &2RMB&r will cast your currently selected spell.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest48CB: A valuable upgrade item used to expand the limits of your spellbook slots.
To upgrade your spell capacity, combine a &aSpell Book&r and any &aSpell Slot Improvement&r item on the &bArcane Anvil&r.
Improves Spell Book capacity, up to 15 slots!
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest48E7: A heavy, waterlogged tome containing the immense and destructive magic of the deep ocean.
Can be obtained by slaying the massive and dangerous &bHullbreaker&r.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest53DC: Essential magical fluids used for crafting and refining your scrolls.
The quality of the ink directly affects the power of the scrolls created, and some higher-tier scrolls will strictly require high-quality ink.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest5626: The primary tools for holding and casting your collection of discovered spells.
All basic spell books can be crafted from a combination of vanilla and arcane materials.
Each further tier crafted increases the slot count of the spell book and often comes with extra bonuses, increasing the power and quantity of spells it can contain.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest588B: A &dSpellbook&r in the main slot and a Scroll of your choice.
The primary workstation for managing your arcane arsenal.
The scrolls you find on your adventures can be inscribed directly into your spellbook using this table!
This is where you add your skills to the book!
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest639F: The base material and the foundation for starting your journey into the arcane.
After you understand the basics of this material, please choose a specific magic class and progress with it.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest658C: The spells inside the book are initially locked but can be improved by combining the book in the &bArcane Anvil&r with a higher-level scroll of the same spell.
A dangerous tome packed with the aggressive summoning and combat magic of the Illagers.
Can be dropped by &cEvokers&r or their more powerful cousin, &cArchevokers&r.
A book full of foundational illager magicl
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest699C: A sacred book brimming with nature magic and ancient forest blessings.
The book may be blessed anew if a proper remedy were applied to
A strong magical energy still persists within the Rotten Spell Book.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest6A71: A powerful workstation used to enhance your magical equipment.
Using ink and scrolls, you can increase the level of your spells to maximize their potential.
It also allows you to improve your gear with upgrade orbs and increase the overall capacity of your spellbooks.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest7A92: The essential workstation used to craft and create new spells.
Allows you to forge powerful magic scrolls using raw magical resources.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest7CCA: A legendary archive holding the dark, forgotten magic of the deep ocean.
This incredibly rare text can only be acquired as a drop by defeating the terrifying &dLeviathan&r.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.quest7F36: A scorched guide containing the volatile methods of high-tier fire casting.
The trick is killing &cBlazes&r and hoping this manual doesn't turn into ash along with them.

An ancient book holding a host of dark spells.
ftbquests.chapter.irons_spells.questA09D: &7What may have once been a more&r

ftbquests.chapter.l2_h.quest14C3ED2C7911: This mod introduces difficulty scaling.. but don't be scared. It is not that bad, trust me.
&cThe more mobs and bosses you defeat, the tougher the game becomes.&r
As your difficulty rises, mobs may spawn with extra &ntraits&r, making each encounter more challenging.&r
You can check your current difficulty by clicking the zombie head icon at the top of your inventory screen.&r
Your adaptive level grows as you keep grinding and slaying mobs.&r
But don’t worry! If you &cdie&r, the difficulty will drop by an major amount. You decide if that's a bad thing or not.&r
And if things get too overwhelming, sip a &bBottle of Sanity&r to ease the difficulty.&r

ftbquests.chapter.lightning_element.ques: Imbue yourself with the power of lightning, increasing your speed and combat abilities.
Charge
Hold to manifest a cone of electrical energy, damaging creatures in its path.
Electrocute
Hold to charge a spear made of lightning. Release to throw it like a javelin, dealing high damage to the creature it hits.
Lightning Lance
Start a chain of lightning from a targeted creature, causing waves of zapping magic to cascade to nearby creatures.
Chain Lightning
Conjure an orb of lightning that slowly moves forward, damaging creatures it phases through and bouncing off blocks.
Ball Lightning
The Lightning school is centered around concentrated damage.
Lightning magic
Coalesce a surge of electrical power, and unleash it in a devastating shockwave, blasting exposed creatures.
Shockwave
Cast to strike lightning at your feet and ascend quickly into the air, and gain reduced gravity for the next few seconds.
Ascension
Down from the heavens, summon a bolt of lightning.
Lightning Bolt
Cast to conjure a thunderstorm centered around yourself, which strikes nearby creatures within sight with a small bolt of lightning every 2 seconds for the spell's duration.
Thunderstorm

ftbquests.chapter.linggango_cut_content_: Add flair to your trains with decorative accessories!
§72. Flowerpots for a touch of nature
§73. Custom banners with your railway logo
§74. Item displays showing cargo or destinations
§75. Trapdoors and fences for detailed exteriors
§7Tip: Small details make a big difference in the appearance of your trains!
Lets you see through the eyes of a conductor as well as control their movement.
calling your train to your location
Same as the Andesite Track Switch but also allows for switching for scheduled trains.
Conductors allow you to activate redstone whilst looking at them and can also carry your toolbox around.
make sure to use headlights too
Steam 'n' Rails adds semaphores - visual indicators for your train signals.
§71. Place them adjacent to track signals
§73. They'll provide clear visual feedback about signal status
§7Tip: Semaphores make it easier to see signal status from a distance, especially useful for complex networks!
The Track Coupler allows you to connect trains together.
Creating themed train cars adds personality to your railway system.
§71. Dining car with tables and food displays
§72. Luxury sleeping car with beds and decorative items
§73. Mail car with chests and item sorters
§74. Observation car with glass roof and panoramic views
§7Tip: Use different wood types, colored glass, and decorative blocks to create distinct themes!
or just to ram through everything (u cant)
Lets you couple and decouple trains without dissasembly.
Brass switches allow for changing track directions from a distance.
§72. Connect to redstone signals if desired
§73. They can be activated by approaching trains

ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest1: This augment allows for precise control over how many items may be sent to or pulled from an inventory, or the router's buffer.

When a module has a Regulator Augment, you will see an extra numeric textfield in its graphical user interface (GUI) where you can configure a limit. This limit is interpreted differently depending on the particular module:

For the Sender Mk1/Mk2/Mk3 Modules and the Player Module (in insert mode), the module will only send an item to the inventory if there are fewer than the configured number of that item already present.
For the Puller Mk1/Mk2 and Player Module (in extract mode), the module will only pull an item from the inventory if there are more than the configured number of that item in the inventory.
&8[Click to read]

This powerful module acts like a fake player which can use an item in the router's buffer, against nearby blocks or entities. There are three modes, which can be selected via the controls on the right of the GUI.
Here's a list of the modes:
&eRight-Click:

This mode performs a right-click with the item in the router's buffer, just as if a player had done so. The logic used is the same as for player clicks, e.g. right-clicking a Flint and Steel on a door will open the door, not start a fire.
&eRight-Click Entity:
Right-click Entity This mode performs a Right-Click with the item in the router's buffer on a nearby entity. You could use this to milk a cow for example, or shear a sheep.
&eAttack Entity:
This mode performs a left-click with the item in the router's buffer on a nearby entity, attacking it. This mode costs Forge Energy by default, unlike other modules.
&8[Click to read]
This augment can be added to any module that has a range, such as the Sender Mk1/Mk2, Puller Mk1/Mk2, Extruder Mk1/Mk2, Fluid Mk2, and Vacuum modules.
It can be stacked, and each augment will reduce the range of the module by one block. This is probably only useful in conjunction with the Vacuum Module to control the range it scans for items.
&8[Click to read]
This augment can only be added to a Vacuum Module.

When this augment is installed, the Vacuum Module will absorb experience orbs instead of items. The orbs will be converted into Bottles o' Enchanting at a rate of 7 XP per bottle. This rate corresponds to the average amount of XP gained by breaking a Bottle o' Enchanting.

Depending on what other mods are installed, it might also be possible to convert orbs into XP fluids. This requires either a fluid-containing tank to be in the router's buffer or adjacent to the router. In the latter case, ensure that the Eject button is enabled in the Vacuum Module's graphical user interface (GUI).
&8[Click to read]
Modular Router is going to be the core of your whole modular router system. but it won't work out of the box, we need modules to make it work.
Let's start with the basic ones.
&8[Click to read]
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest2: Put it in the modular router
This module extends and withdraws a row of blocks (from the router's buffer) in the module's configured direction.
Using this module, its possible to build large extending bridges and doorways.

This upgrade is used to guarantee that two or morerouters run at the same time. The Sync Upgrade has a tuning value of 0 .. 19 (i.e. 1 less than the base router tick rate of 20). This tuning value can be set by right-clicking the Sync Upgrade to open its GUI, or quickly set to a random value by sneak-right-clicking the upgrade.

Any routers with this upgrade installed, with same the tuning value, and with the same number of Speed Upgrades installed, will always run on the same tick. The actual tuning value doesn't matter; only that the value is consistent across the routers you want to synchronise.
This is particularly useful if you have a group of routers with Extruder Modules installed, and want to ensure all modules extend/retract at exactly the same time.
Disguise your router as a another block.
This augment can be added to any module.

Normally, modules are run when the router runs, with no questions asked. However, if this augment is added to a module, that module can define its own redstone behavior. An extra button is added to the module's graphical user interface (GUI) to control this setting.
Modules in a router operate on a single item at a time: e.g. a Sender Module will send a single item from the router's buffer each router tick, regardless of how many items are in the buffer.

By adding Stack Upgrades to a router, this can be increased. Each Stack Upgrade doubles the number of items that can be processed by each module, up to a maximum of 64, or the item's native stack size (e.g. 16 for Ender Pearls).
It therefore follows that 6 is the maximum number of useful Stack Upgrades which can be installed in one router.
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest3: Modular routers mod offers HUGE amount of modules that can make your storage system even cleaner, and better!
Last tier of sender module, can transfer items up to INFINITE amount of range!
Can send items to multiple targets, pretty much a extended version of sender mk.2

This module behaves like the Dropper Module, except that it also imparts a configurable velocity to the dropped item, potentially throwing the item a considerable distance. You can ad just the item's speed, pitch and yaw via the module's GUI.
This augment can only be applied to the Extruder Mk1 and Extruder Mk2 modules.
It can be stacked. Each instance of the augment increases the force with which entities are shoved when the module extrudes a block.
This augment can be added to any module that handles items (as opposed to blocks, fluids, or energy).

Adding Stack Augments to a module increases the number of items it can process per tick. Each Stack Augment doubles the number of items, up to a maximum of the item's natural stack size (which is 64 for most items).

If the router also has Stack Upgrades installed, the module's Stack Augments will override that setting. For example, if a router has 6 Stack Upgrades, and a module has 2 Stack Augments, the module will process 4 items per tick, not 64.
This augment can only be added to the Extruder Module Mk2.
When present, any fake blocks extruded by the module take on the properties of the faked block, e.g. Obsidian is hard to break, Glowstone emits light, and Redstone Blocks emit a redstone signal.
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest4: Put it in the modular router
By default, a router ticks every 20 server ticks, or once per second. On each tick, every installed module is executed in order, left to right.
If you need a router to run faster than this, add Speed Upgrades; each Speed Upgrade reduces the tick interval by 2 server ticks, down to aminimum of every 2 server ticks (or 10 times per second).

A Router with a Security Upgrade installed can only be  opened by players whitelisted by that upgrade. The upgrade doesn't prevent a router being broken; but since upgrades and modules remain in a broken router, players can't break a router to steal modules/upgrades or reconfigure the router. If you want to prevent a router being broken, you may wish to explore other mods' block protection capabilities.

Several modules (Placer, Breaker, Activator) do their work as a fake player. By default this is a player with the username [Modular Routers], but when a security upgrade is installed, those modules operate as the upgrade's owner. This is much safer on multiplayer servers with claim protection than simply allowing [Modular Routers] to do anything with your claims.

When the Activator Module is in Attack Mode, it will ignore any players whitelisted by a Security Upgrade installed in the Router. This is generally a good idea to avoid pain \& suffering if you have a high-damage weapon in your Router.
This module behaves somewhat like the Extruder Module Mk1, but with a few important differences:
The Mk2 module doesn't place items from the router's buffer, but rather from a template defined within the module itself.
This template appears on the right-hand side of the module's GUI, has 9 slots, and can have multiple items per slot. Items placed in the template are "ghost items.
Blocks extruded are "virtual" - effectively created out of nowhere, but drop nothing when broken and can't be crafted normally.
Therefore, this module isn't suitable for farming like the Extruder Mk1, but can be used to place doors/bridges etc. with more complex patterns than the Mk1 could.
The default range for the Extruder Mk2 is 24 blocks, which can be increased to 48 with Range Upgrades.
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest5: Unlike the Mk.1, the Mk.2 puller requires a right-click to select the target container for item transfer.
Second tier of sender, can send items up to 24 blocks.
Makes the router immune to explosion.
Increases overall fluid transfer rate.

This module doesn't actually manipulate items, but instead detects specific items in the router's buffer. If the buffer contents are matched by the module's filter, it will make the router emit a redstone signal in the configured direction.
&8[Click to read]
Unlike the Mk.1, the Mk.2 puller requires a right-click to specify the target storage for item extraction.
Latest tier of puller, can pull from 12 blocks away.
This module tries to transfer items between the router and a player's own inventory. The player can be anywhere in any dimension, making this a rather powerful module.
The module can transfer items either from or to the player; you can set this in the module's GUI.

The module can operate on either the player's main 36- slot inventory, their 4-slot armor inventory, their offhand slot, or their Ender inventory (as shown by vanilla Ender Chests). This can also be set in the module's GUI.
Extracted/inserted items can, of course, be filtered by the module.
&8[Click to read]

This augment can only be added to the Vacuum Module. It allows the Vacuum Module to ignore the normal pickup delay on dropped items - 40 ticks for items dropped by players, and 10 ticks for items dropped by other means (e.g. sheep shearing) - and pick up items as soon as it sees them.
A max of one Fast Pickup Augment can be added.
&8[Click to read]
Upgrades are huge QoL mechanics, i heavily suggest you to use them!
Upgrades
Transfers Fluids, has 12 block range.
This module tries to break blocks..
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest6: This augment can be added to any module that has a range, including the Sender Mk1/Mk2, Puller Mk1/Mk2, Extruder Mk1/Mk2, Fluid Mk2, and Vacuum modules.
It can be stacked, and each augment will increase the range of the module by one block.
&eSender&r sends items to a another storage method, always has more range than puller.
&ePuller&r has short range, pulls items from the storage and sends it to sender.
Remember these are only mk.1 in other module tiers they get really op quickly.
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.quest7: In order to set up your modular router system, you have to configure your modules.

While they're &ein the modular router,&r Middle-Click your modules. This will open a another GUI showing you where to extract the item to. Choose the direction your storage is facing and it will Pull \& Send.
Sender mk.1 works up to 8 blocks of range, so just keep the direction correct.
aaandd congratulations, you have set your first modular routers system up!
&8More information:

&8There are nine filter slots, where you can insert ghost copies of any item or block. The module will only operate if the item in the router's buffer matches an item in the module's filter (if whitelisting; the opposite if blacklisting).
{image:modularrouters:textures/patchouli/module_gui.png width:100 height:100 align:center}
&8[Click to read]
okay
Setting up your first system
This augment can only be added to the Dropper and Flinger modules.

It can be stacked; each augment added will increase the pickup delay on dropped/flung items by 10 ticks (0.5 sec). This can be useful if you don't want players accidentally picking up nearby dropped items, for example.
&8[Click to read]

This module tries to place an item from the router's buffer as a block, adjacent to the router in the module's configured direction. If the item isn't a block or the destination block space is obstructed, nothing will be done.
Normal Minecraft placement rules are followed; e.g. sugar cane can only be placed next to water on sand/dirt.
Fluid blocks will be replaced, as will replaceable blocks such as tall grass.
The Placer module can plant seeds on farmland, and cocoa beans on an adjacent jungle log.
&8[Click to read]

This module attempts to output FE (Forge Energy) from the Router to an adjacent energy- receiving block, in its configured direction. The module can extract energy from either the router's internal energy buffer (if any Energy Upgrades are installed) or from an energy-containing item in the router's item buffer. The internal energy buffer is used by preference.
&8[Click to read]
This module scans for dropped items in a 13x13x13 cubic area around the router (i.e. up to 6 blocks in each direction), and absorbs them into the router's buffer, if possible.
&8[Click to read]

This module tries to drop an item from the router's buffer as an item entity in the world. The drop will be ad jacent to the router in the module's configured direction. The item entity will be placed with a zero velocity, unlike the vanilla Dropper which adds an irritating random velocity to the item.
&8[Click to read]
This dangerous module permanently destroys items in the router's buffer!
It is strongly recommended to configure this module with a whitelist filter to prevent accidental deletion of valuable items.
&8[Click to read]
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.questB: This module is like a combination of an Energy Output and a Distributor Module, in that it can wirelessly distribute FE (Forge Energy) to up to 8 nearby energy-accepting blocks.
ftbquests.chapter.modular_routers.questF: Gives the router an internal energy buffer.

ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest1C: The Nature school is focused on applying debuffs to enemies, with elements of damage to finish them off.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest27: Spew forth a cone of poison, damaging and poisoning creatures in its reach.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest38: Target a creature to blight them, reducing their incoming healing and outgoing damage.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest44: Spit an orb of acid, splashing in a radius upon impact and rending creatures hit, melting away and reducing their armor.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest6C: Turn your stomach into an arcane furnace, turning eaten food into mana for a duration.
Around a targeted creature, sprout magical roots than entangle them and completely prevent non-magical movement.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest6F: Instill yourself with poisonous magic, dealing additional melee, ranged, or spell damage creatures that are poisoned.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest72: Around a targeted creature or block, conjure a wave of poisonous magic that splashes down, damaging creatures hit and leaving behind a poisonous cloud.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest75: Charge a powerful arrow, imbued with poison magic, that will release a poisonous cloud upon impact.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest7E: The great power of oak infuses itself into your skin, granting damage reduction of any incoming damage for a duration.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.quest7F: Emanate a swarm of violent fireflies, following a targeted creature and dealing aoe damage. If the initial target dies, the last harmed creature will be targeted.
ftbquests.chapter.nature_element.title: <grad from=#30FF68 to=#B6FCC9>Nature Element</grad>

ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest12: When slimes split into smaller versions, they get consumed and converted into aura!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest16: By stunting the growth of large trees and only allowing small trees to grow, the canopy diminisher releases a large amount of aura into the environment.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest18: When starting out as a magical botanist, it proves &ebeneficial&r to use the powers of nature on a more direct level before delving directly into &dAura&r itself.
Down below, there's a visual examples of the &aRitual Of Forest&r
The ritual happens when the tree grows. Remember to recheck the recipe to see which tree is planted!
&cBe careful, some biomes may affect oak tree generation. This will affect the ritual.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest19: &n&lFor item placement&r&r, you can use hoppers to put items onto wooden stands.

&l&nFor dust placement&r&r, outline the areas of the gold powder placement with a type of decorative block like stone bricks, hold the gold powder in your off-hand, place them with a construction wand.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest1C: &cLow&r Aura levels will cause leaves to decay on their own.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest1D: Store excess aura and use them on the go!
Hold it in your curio slot and travel around the world, it'll fill itself up in aura dense places!
You'll know it's charging when it bounces in your hand!
One quick and totally not recommended way of filling it is letting the natural altar charge it for you...
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest1F: Huge water storage that works with Aura
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest22: Nature's Aura is a mod about collecting, using and replenishing the Aura naturally present in the world to create useful devices and unique mechanics.
&8I don't like this mod, but i will admit. No nvm, i still don't like this mod.
If this questline feels vague, i suggest using the official guide book. It is well detailed and even has a visualizer for the multi-block structures.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest23: Use various methods to get items inside the Offering Table. Droppers can drop Catalysts onto the table and activate it! Be carefule because resulting items may fall onto your contraption!
If only there is a device to transfer redstone wirelessly...
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest26: When aura in the area usually runs out and won't replenish, Ancient Trees slowly replenish the aura in the area. This is why they're crucial!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest31: Makes snow fall in a area with the supply of aura and signal of redstone
The block below the altar is left empty in order for you to automatically pull results out with a hopper!
Another hopper can be used to put items inside the altar, very convenient!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest33: Aura bottles can be automatically collected using a dispenser
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest34: Breed animals in aura dense places
Convert tainted gold or infused iron ingot into Ingot of the Skies
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest37: Too &ahigh&r levels of Aura on the area can cause grass to grow on netherrack.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest43: This section of the quest line is only visible when you or one of your teammates walk on the path of righteousness.
This &lDOES NOT&r act as what you must complete, rather a checklist of what you can make following the quest line that somewhat reduces the burdens of players wearing The Ring of Seven Virtues.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest45: Place it next to a device that harnesses Aura.
Shooting Mark generates aura when being shot with projectiles.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest47: Aura imbalance can happen if you have either &aTOO much Aura&r or &cTOO low Aura&r
These two factors can cause positive \& negative effects on you.
If Aura levels are &4&lVERY LOW&r to the point where natural decay has already happened to it's utmost. Explosions will start to appear on your world.
By the help of &aCreational Catalyst&r Aura levels can go beyond limits. Causing ores to appear in stones, netherracks.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest4A: Store more aura than you will ever need!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest4B: The most basic catalyst, throw it on the ground when you want to make offers to the gods
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest4F: Bottled substance you see in recipes are any types of bottled aura (sunlight, ghost, etc.)
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest52: Right click Bottle and Cork in the Nether
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest54: &cLow&r Aura on the area can cause netherrack to decay.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest55: Right click Bottle and cork in the Overworld
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest56: &aVery High&r Aura levels will cause vegetation to grow on the area.
&cLow&r Aura on the area can cause mobs to suffocate, and die.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest58: Uses Aura to destroy surrounding blocks.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest5A: Place the filled Aura Trove onto the Offering Table, and drop the spirit of birthing nearby, and you'll receive this
With Nature's Mend enchantment, you can repair your tools and armor by having the aura cache/ trove in your inventory, hold the item in your hand, then crouch to repair it on the go!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest5E: Additionally eggs that are on the ground will hatch.
Too &ahigh&r Aura levels in the area can cause animals to breed uncontrollably.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest60: Herbivorous Absorber, absorbs mana from the flowers.
Basically, you can feed it flowers and it will provide aura!
Don't use the same flower for too long though, it may have negative effects.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest62: - 1x Raw Beef or Raw Porkchop or Raw Chicken
Prepare a large enough field, decorate the offering table with beautiful flowers and the omniscient gods might answer your prayers...
Place materials onto the table (maximum 16, 1 if the item is non-stackable) and throw the catalyst nearby, and your prayers will rain from the sky!
Make sure the surrounding areas and the sky access is clear, for items might fall on top of the roof otherwise!
&4DISCLAIMER:&r This image is only for visualization, you DO NOT need to do this on the nether roof.
Cloudy with a chance of... items?
Swamp Homi collect moss from blocks and restores them, in this process it even produces aura!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest69: When powered on with a &credstone&r signal- stops mobs from appearing in a 48 block radius
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest6E: Transmutes items into different forms, actually useful
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest71: Produces Aura by killing &epassive&r mobs near it
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest73: Right click Bottle and cork in aura-deprived areas
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest78: Alright, let's start with nature-ifying items.
Natural Altar is our first way of harnessing aura that is rudimentary but still works.
&8If you are having problems tryna understand, just use the guidebook. It has a visualization feature.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest79: Cloudshiter changes the wheather with the help of aura.
Throwing some flowers can decide on the weather type:
Throwing more items also increases the amount of time that the desired weather will most likely remain for, but also increases the Aura usage of the shift.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest7A: &cLow&r Aura levels on the area can cause neutral mobs to be hostile at all time. (like enderman)
Makes hopper grab items from 7 blocks instead of 1.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest7D: Right click Bottle and cork in the End
Doubles the ore output in blast furnaces.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest7F: Keeps the chunks always active, may cause problems though. Be careful!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest8C: A natural occurence that helped the discovery of Aura are certain kinds of vegetation that appear almost everywhere in the world.
Aura is the power source of this mod and we will need it in everything!
There's ton of ways to gather aura so let's start.
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest9A: Brilliant fiber turns leaves into golden leaves, allowing you to extract the leaves and then use it for other stuff.
Use it on a tree with a lot of leaves!
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.quest9B: Right click holding the bottle will collect aura from the environment
If I can store aura in a bottle...
ftbquests.chapter.natures_aura_2.title: <shadow a=0.4><grad from=#e6d520 to=#C0AF27>Nature's Aura</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest18CA053: The more damage you take, the scarier this necklace gets.
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest34DD86C: Pops a bubble shield that protects you from harm.
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest47CE07C: This necklace does.. alot of things i am lazy so yeah
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest485D860: You were destined for something greater, you had a greater purpose.
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest4B012E2: Sometimes running is an better option than fighting.
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest57F321F: Basically, a spam attack wont work on you (a lil)
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest6066C19: Zapp those who touch the almighty zeus!
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest6422232: You do not sink in water, whoever touches you gets hurt and u can paralyze
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest6D48B15: You are going to reach heaven, keep going.
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.quest7976369: Water doesnt slow you down while walking now!
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.questD51E42F: Burn those who touch your beautiful holy skin!
ftbquests.chapter.necklaces.title: Necklaces

ftbquests.chapter.powah.image.hovertext7: Unauthorized use or theft of this content is strictly prohibited and protected under copyright law.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest12C552C76EC: Ender Cells are the highlight of this mod, alongside Ender Gates.
Store energy inside and use it wirelessly using ender gates, anywhere!
Capacity is increased by adding Batteries which get consumed.
Make sure that power is being stored inside the right channel, and that the gates are set to the right channel as well.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest16A4C7E5276: The solar panel is the lowest energy generator alongside the thermo generator, but its pretty simple to set up. Place the panel in an open space and it will generate power as long as its day and its not obstructed by any block.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest1E62C8701AB: The energizing rod is used to power the orb by being placed near it, and you can also use multiple of them. It requires a power source so make sure its connected to a power generation system.
I recommend making at least 2-3 for faster crafting speed.
You can use the wrench to configure the rod to power a specific orb if there are multiple ones next to each other.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest1F1D7F220D1: The Binding Card can be binded to a player by right-clicking with it in your hand. This can be placed in the Player Transmitter to power any item or equipment in your inventory, as long as you are in the same dimension as the transmitter.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest291E9301F3E: The power of thunder, in your hands.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest31E9AE81FB8: The Player Transmitter is used to charge the player's equipment and items wirelessly. This can be done by connecting the transmitter to a power source, and putting a Binding Card inside it.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest3A313AF01E7: The magmator generates power using lava. Make sure you have a stable supply.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest4161BC5BBC9: The Reactor is a multiblock and the best power generator that this mod has to offer. You will require 36 reactor blocks to build it, and this is automatic. Place one block where you want it to be built, then right click it with the blocks in your main hand. This will build the multiblock in a 3x4 area, so make sure its clear.

The fuel used to power the reactor is Uraninite, and nothing else. The reactor can also be augmented using redstone and coal in their designated slots. Doing this will make the heat rise, which will increase the fuel consumption, alonside power generation. To cool it down you will require a coolant, which can be liquid or solid. This means ice blocks and/or a water source. For specific values and more information check the &nManual&r.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest45A3458B67A: Used to pick up, config and rotate Powah blocks and cables.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest4694C207FEE: The Manual has all of the information you need about the blocks and items of this mod, so if you ever need a quick look at what something does or its stats, check it!
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest486EE3BEE91: The thermo generator is a pretty interesting one. It requires water input to cool down and function, as well as a heat source under it. This can be a magma block or lava. Electricity output depends on the heat source used, lava being the highest one.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest4C9BB0FA98D: The Energy Discharger is used to drain the power from items put inside it and store it in an internal buffer, which then can be transferred or stored. This works only with Batteries or Cells.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest4DB10CDB42E: Just energy cables. Configurable using the &nWrench&r.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest4ECA971BF00: Using the newly created dielectric paste and quite a bit of iron, we can finally make the first materials required to get into this mod.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest505A752D6BA: The first step in creating your first &ePowah &fgenerator is making some &ndielectric paste&r. This does require you to get to the nether, so put your fireproof pants on and go slaying!
Dielectric paste will also be required in almost all recipes, so make sure you stock well on this.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest545200654EF: The furnator uses fuel like coal to generate power.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest57122BCD68F: The Energy Hopper can charge all the items in the chest or block with an inventory that its connected to, when supplied with power. This works only on Batteries or Cells.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest5A2DDE5D50D: &ePowah&r is a mod that adds high &benergy &fgeneration options, including a multiblock, as well as a wireless way to transport said energy. Yes, thats right, &lwireless transfer &rof energy which does not have a range limit at all!
It also introduces a way to wirelessly charge all your equipment and items, even across dimensions, for as long as your power source does not run out.
This questline will guide you through all you need to know about this mod, so make sure you check it regularly.
&8This questline was made by &e@cristoic&r &8in our discord server, thanks for his amazing work!
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest6413B1F6B45: This is the "crafting bench" you will use to progress through this mod. Be warned, higher tier materials will require more power to craft, which can get very high very fast. Check JEI or manual for values.
You can automate the orb, although it can be a bit tricky. For an in-depth tutorial check the links below.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest6EAAE060256: By using a photoelectric pane on an enderman or endermite, you can obtain the Lens of Ender. This can be used on a solar panel to make it generate power even through blocks.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest7750A55ECB0: Can store and carry energy, which will charge equipment and items as long as its in your inventory. When added to an Ender Cell it will get consumed and increase the cell's capacity. Any power stored in the battery will get added to the network once consumed.
Be careful which channel you add the Batteries to in the Ender Cell. This action cannot be reversed.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest7AA9032ACFD: The Ender Gates transfer power from the Ender Cell network. Wirelessly! Just stick them to the machine or cable you want to power and make sure its set to the right channel. Don't forget you need power inside the Ender Cell to be able to transfer it ;). Higher tiers transfer power faster.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest7B0A4FE2724: The Dimensional Binding Card allows charging across dimensions. Obtained by right-clicking an Enderman or Endermite with a binding card.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest7BAD04C4928: Basic energy storage. To not be confused with the Ender Cell.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.quest95DD556225D: Uraninite is an ore that can be found underground at y level 64 or lower. Its found in small veins but the mined amount can be increased using Fortune.
ftbquests.chapter.powah.title: <shadow><grad from=#3f3f45 to=#2d2d30>Powah</grad></shadow>

ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest1116B72E: Nyxaris is a ruthless entity born of pure malice and the ultimate challenge of your journey.
Are you truly prepared for what he &4brings&r?
&eEverything you have learned and mastered throughout this entire progression line will be required to survive this fight.&r
To help manage the mechanics of this battle and ease the brutal difficulty, you must bring the &nAbsorbing Stone&r with you into the arena. Good luck.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest14F57F76: You have unlocked &6elvenite&r armors. To see all of them, click on the item icon.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest157136D6: You have unlocked &aemerald&r armors. To see all of them, click on the item icon.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest177DFA9C: To progress further, your next destination is the Undergarden. Crafting the next required boss item demands &cUtherium&r, a resource found exclusively within this deep, subterranean dimension.

To enter, construct a portal frame out of &bStone Bricks&r, using the same shape as a standard Nether portal. However, instead of using a regular flint and steel, you must ignite the frame using a specific &bCatalyst&r.
&8Refer to the dedicated Undergarden questline for more information on surviving this dimension.&r
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest19F19149: Unlike standard Minecraft progression, which requires only one type of eye to activate the End Portal, Linggango necessitates several unique eyes to fill the frame.
Please keep these constraints in mind:
- Unique Eyes Required: You must provide &ndistinct&r types of eyes. Duplicates don't work!
- Drop Rates: Endermen have a &e0.5%%&r chance to drop these unique eyes.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest1A27B4B4: You have unlocked &e&l&ohe&r&6&l&oli&r&e&l&oon&r&6&l&oite&r tools. To see all of them, click on the item icon.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest1ABCE51A: &cNegates healing&r when obtained. It can be crafted into splash potions or tipped arrows.
Melee weapons may also carry an enchantment that applies the curse, known as &cCursed Blade&r.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest1BE503F8: Fighting against one of the overworld's greatest, the ancient one.
With enough strength, and keen judgement of which to prioritize, and evade, you will be able to easily triumph through.
Use what you've learned in your previous experiences, and it shall end up in the same result.
&eAll of thy preparation, has come to this moment.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest1C546D49: This time, the obstacle you'll face is a tougher opponent, capable of inflicting distracting  effects that will make this journey difficult, avoid those,  and you will easily triumph through.
Perhaps the &ebow of the ancient sage&r may help you the most with this task, although you can resort to using your &epure brute strength&r, or &eother methods &rthat the virtues path provides.
This obstacle will test your wisdom of knowing which one to prioritize, the bigger one? or its summon instead?
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest1D3C73CC: Now that you've fought some of the tough challenges, are you ready to fight someone from the ancient times?
Has your observation and thought process improved?
Fighting one of the ancients will test out what you've learned so far.
&4Avoid the trouble that comes from him and his harbingers&r, and you'll see yourself succeeding through.
Lets put that to the test, shall we?
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest2222AD8D: What is this quest for? You may ask...
&8why are you asking, I already answered it.
Your next boss fight, just like every other one. Is not a brain-dead m1 spam.
-Spawn this boss near your base, spawn it farther.
-Bring an elytra with you&r, especially for 2nd phase.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest231B0621: &eElvenite&r is a powerful custom ore introduced by the Armageddon mod. Defeating this boss will grant you full access to crafting and utilizing &eElvenite tools and equipment&r.

This encounter features a severe durability-shredding mechanic. The boss inflicts massive durability damage to your equipped armor and weapons. Crucially, the boss's own &ndamage scaling increases&r for every point of durability your gear loses during the fight.
Additionally, watch your strikes carefully; the boss utilizes a &creflect&r mechanic that can turn your own high damage back against you, ending the encounter instantly.

&eStrategy Tip:&r It is highly recommended to make your primary gear &eunbreakable&r or apply the &eHardened&r enchantment before attempting this challenge to completely neutralize the boss's scaling power.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest23B4C927: The challenge will demand a choice of priorities.
Will you strike the boss until his servants arrive, or keep pressing forward without &cfear&r?
Remember, &cthese servants are more dangerous than the boss itself&r, so choose your focus carefully.
Your decision matters, and the consequences will follow every wrong step.
&cThe agony, resumes for a second time.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest25326B96: Seems to be heavily inspired by Mohg, Lord of Blood from Elden Ring..
This boss possesses dangerous life-drain and area-of-effect mechanics.
The boss is capable of &dregenerating&r his health continuously as long as his blood orbs remain active on the battlefield.
Prioritize destroying these orbs immediately to halt his healing.
Additionally, keep a close eye on the passive &cblood aura&r surrounding him, which deals constant damage every few seconds.
The blood orbs themselves also damage the area directly around them, which is marked by a clear visual indicator.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest297F31BB: &cZoranth&r is a fierce warrior who once fought against the armies of the Nether. Defeating this powerful boss breaks your next major progression lock, granting you full access to &8Netherite&r weapons and armor.
Zoranth can only be summoned and fought within the Nether dimension.

Zoranth cannot take damage until all of his active summons are defeated. He will also continuously regenerate his health throughout the fight. To stop this regeneration, you must apply an anti-heal mechanic by keeping him afflicted with the &ccursed&r status effect, which you can apply via potions or the Cursed Blade enchantment.

This encounter features severe negative status effects, particularly anti-heal. While Cleanse Potions and Milk Buckets will remove these dangerous conditions, they will also strip away your positive active buffs. Utilizing cleanse spells is a highly effective alternative to clear out the debuffs without losing your own status advantages.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest2B69C369: &dThe Ender Dragon&r is one of the most ancient beings in existence, guarding the gateway to the outer End Cities until the very end.
During the battle, she unleashes a constant barrage of high-damage projectiles. Getting hit by these will severely chunk your health bar, so continuous movement is essential.
Upon her defeat, she will drop a rare and vital material known as &eKhyros's Tears&r.
&c&nWARNING:&r Unlike other encounters, defeating the Ender Dragon will permanently shift your world's balance, unlocking new scaling mechanics and dangers.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest2BD02C0C: This attribute pierces entirely through any armor rating, applying consistently to both physical and magical damage types.
This makes it exceptionally useful in almost any late-game combat scenario.
&8Note: Defeating entities using this mechanic will not yield standard hostility loot drops.&r
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest2EAC826E: &cEldorath&r is an ancient warrior whose prime was long ago, but do not underestimate him. He remains incredibly powerful.

During the battle, he will force you to levitate and launch dangerous projectiles that you must dodge. He also summons hostile Allays, which you should prioritize clearing out immediately. You can actually destroy his incoming projectiles by attacking them directly mid-air, helping you stay safe from both the boss and his summons.
Defeating Eldorath unlocks a powerful new arsenal of weapons, access to core enchanting mechanics, and the key to &bDiamond&r crafting.
&cImportant:&r Due to the levitation trait, make sure to bring a water bucket, Bunny Hoppers, or any curio that provides fall damage negation.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest2FC648D9: This monster forces you into a state of &6constant hunger&r, rapidly draining your food bar throughout the fight.
In addition to the starvation threat, it features a reflection mechanic that inflicts direct back-damage to you every single time you land a hit.
Balance your health and food carefully before playing aggressively.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest338DD0DD: Defeating this entity is critical, as it drops a fundamental key item required to trigger all upcoming boss encounters in the pack.
Be warned that this foe becomes completely immortal for a short duration during its second phase. You will need to play defensively and wait for the status to expire before resuming your assault.
However, if your setup utilizes a &bSoul Flame Blade&r, &bIce Blade&r, or &bSharp Blade&r, your attacks can actually bypass this immortality phase entirely.
&8From this point forward, the hand-holding is over. It is time to optimize your own custom build for what lies ahead.&r

ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest3F362F10: This entity remains completely hidden from the naked eye, meaning you will need an &8L2 Detector&r or a similar tracking utility just to locate and target it.

Getting hit by this boss triggers a dangerous mechanic where &dyour enchantments can get sealed&r, temporarily disabling your gear's active modifiers. If you are struggling with this effect, check the &oL2 Hostility&r questline for full details on how to manage enchantment sealing.

ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest41964C08: Additionally, the &eIncarceration&r status effect is incredibly effective here for locking him in place. Look into utilizing a specific &ering&r or &epotion effect&r capable of applying this condition to completely control his movement.
Like every other major encounter, your next boss fight requires strategy over mindless left-clicking. Unless, of course, you are completely overprepared.
Here are the essential mechanics and strategies you need to know before engaging him:
Vaedric possesses the dangerous &bReprint&r trait. This means he will automatically copy the exact enchantments currently active on your equipped gear.
&8(For a complete breakdown of how this mechanic scales, check the L2 Hostility -> Legendary Traits questline).&r
Vaedric utilizes a powerful magic pull to drag you into danger. Utilizing the &eInsulator&r enchantment on your gear can block this pulling effect entirely, allowing you to maintain your spacing.
If you prefer to freestyle the encounter, you can opt out of the enchantment and instead use his pulling mechanic to your advantage to rapidly close the distance.

ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest4BFFC4F9: The Four Calamities are directly responsible for the current state of the Overworld, serving as the primary reason it is now corrupted and overrun with undead monsters.
Each of them possesses unique strengths, so approach these encounters with extreme caution.

ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest4E97AB0C: Vaedric was once a lone traveler of the End dimension. His journey met a dark end, and he now wields the volatile power of the Void itself.

This power allows him to bring forth &nsummons&r that fight by his side. Exercise extreme caution during the battle, as both Vaedric and his minions have the ability to &npull&r you directly into their attack range.
Avoid relying on damage-over-time effects against this boss. He is capable of completely slipping away from or neutralizing most status-based damage.
Defeating him removes the next major progression lock, granting full access to powerful &bVoiderite&r tier items.
&e&nNote:&r Vaedric must be summoned on the Outer End Islands.

ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest4F34F740: &cArion&r is an &8Elite Pillager&r who wields dangerous magical abilities.

Be extremely careful during his second phase, as he will counter-attack whenever he takes damage. He also features a distinct power-up phase where he begins to glow, significantly increasing his damage output. Watch his tells and take caution when this happens.
Defeating him unlocks a vast new tier of equipment, including access to &2Emerald&r crafting materials.
