ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest5C70A896: Before you can craft Ankh's Tablet, you must first acquire the Tinkerer's Accessory Template.
To create it, you need to harvest three unique types of &nSpirits&r dropped by vanilla mobs: &cDespair, Dread, and Chaos&r.
Note that different mobs (such as Zombies, Slimes, and Skeletons) drop different spirit types.
Once you have gathered the spirits, complete the crafting process:
&b-&r Utilize the collection of Spirits to craft a Calamitous Core.
&b-&r Combine the Calamitous Core with Khyros's Goldened Tear to successfully create the &dAccessory Template&r.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest5D3E8E9B: If you noticed your iron tools aren't working, you have officially hit the first major progression lock.
To unlock the ability to use iron equipment, you must summon and defeat

As the leader of the Iron Golems, this boss emits a passive aura that slows you down when you get close, and his direct hits will slow you even further. Time your strikes carefully, utilize your dodge mechanic, and avoid letting him corner you.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest5DFA8A8C: You have finally &eascended&r and claimed what was meant to be yours from the very beginning.
You have shown the traits and values that embody true virtue.
From this point forward, you must walk your own path and take control of your actions without guidance. The choice is now yours.
If you ever lose your way or struggle to see the road ahead, remember these &nimportant&r artifacts:
&e- The scroll that holds a thousand virtues.&r
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest638BAC3C: This boss is capable of duplicating itself alongside its active summons, rapidly multiplying the threats on the battlefield.
Be on high alert for its &bDispel&r ability. If you allow yourself to get cornered, it will systematically seal your gear's enchantments one by one.
To counter this, look into your enchantment options, as a specific modifier can help suppress the boss and prevent it from splitting entirely.
ftbquests.chapter.progress.quest647CCE4C: Reborn from the power of the sun, Zoranth returns far more powerful than before, securing his spot as one of the ultimate challenges in the pack.

Every boss features unique combat gimmicks, meaning &nproper preparation&r is vital before jumping into the fight. Take your time, gear up, and don't rush in unprepared.

The <grad from=#228B22 to=#AAFF00 f=1>Varasuchus</grad> is a highly adaptive semi-aquatic dragon species known for its phase-shifting combat behavior and deceptive threat presentation.
Raw Cod, Raw Salmon, Tropical Fish, Hearty Dragon Meal.
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Go to the next page to view how to breed them.</grad>

Requires a pair of one male and one female with full health. Shift + Right-click with the appropriate food item under the Taming section to initiate breeding. Should you breed one male dragon that you own with a female dragon that someone else owns, or otherwise, the baby will default to following the female.
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Go to the next page to view their abilities.</grad>
&eThis dragon has the ability to jump like vanilla Horses.&r

&2Hold R&r only on &ePhase 1&r: Tail guard and parry. While holding, Varasuchus will enter a guarding stance, immune to damage. If something attacks it while in guard, it'll perform a parry and knocks everything back while dealing damage.
&2G&r - Tail attack on phase 1. Clawing on phase 2.
&2H&r - Phase Shifting: Phase 2. Stops playing nice. Go from quadruped to bipedal.
&2Double-tap W&r for a dash and tail swiping in &ephase 1&r, and a dash and clawing in &ephase 2&r.

Just feeding him won't be so easy, first you need to lower his health to &c100&r, and then feed him any of the food, it's also not guranteed to tame him first time, if you fail to tame him, you'd have to lower his health again.

To activate &2Phase 2&r once you've tamed one, &2double-tap&r the &2S&r key.
&ePhase 1&r: Left-click: Bite that sets targets of fire.
&2R&r - Roar: Summons three magma pillars three times on the ground. &2Hold R&r to charge up a 3-level fireball. The bigger the level, the stronger the fireball.
&ePhase 1 and 2&r: &2G&r - Fire Breathing: &2Hold&r to shoot a stream of fire. &2Hold&r a little longer to destroy blocks.
&2H&r - Supernova (exaggerated): Releases a big explosion. Nearby mobs get decimated at the cost of health. Phase 2 happens in an instant, no wind-up.
&ePhase 1&r: &2Double-tapping W&r will make Ignivorus enter its bulldozing state. &2Double-tapping&r again to exit.

ftbquests.chapter.saints_dragons.quest4A: ## <grad from=#FC5454 to=#FC5454 f=1><wave a=0.2 f=0.2 w=0.2>Ivy Oleander</wave></grad>
&eShe can be found in the Forest biome, rarely..&r
A trader known for her long history with dragons, especially <grad from=#8B0000 to=#C0392B f=1>Raevyx</grad>.

One time she found <grad from=#8B0000 to=#C0392B f=1>Raevyx's</grad> egg with the corpse of a mother dragon laying nearby, and took it home. Since then, she has been raising her dragon from a young age, and now specializes in trading items for dragon eggs.
She has pale skin, crimson eyes, and dark hair that resembles <grad from=#8B0000 to=#C0392B f=1>Raevyx's</grad> species.
To trade with her, you first need to arrive with an egg, then right-click her and she'll start trusting you, which will unlock her trades.
<typewriter><grad from=#C84443 to=#C84443 f=0.3>I wish I was her little Raevyx.. - tookover</grad>
<grad from=#FC5454 to=#FC5454 f=1><wave a=0.5 f=0.8 w=0.5>Ivy Oleander</wave></grad>

ftbquests.chapter.saints_dragons.quest6A: ## <grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Overview</grad>
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>They can be found in the following biomes:</grad>
[gap=0]
&9Ocean Biomes&r.
[gap=5]
[gap=5]
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Go to the next page to look how to tame them.</grad>
## <grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Taming</grad>
&6Required food&r
Raw Cod, Raw Salmon, Hearty Dragon Meal, Pufferfish, Tropical Fish.
The <grad from=#B7334B to=#42596F f=1>Volitans</grad> is a highly territorial semi-aquatic dragon species adapted for environmental control, area denial, and prolonged territorial occupation.

Just feeding him won't be so easy, first you need to lower his health to &c60&r, and then feed him any of the food, it's also not guranteed to tame him first time, if you fail to tame him, you'd have to lower his health again.
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Go to the next page to view how to breed them.</grad>
## <grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Breeding</grad>

Requires a pair of one male and one female with full health <grad from=#ADD8E6 to=#5454FC f=1><wave a=0.3 f=0.5 w=0.35>while being underwater</wave></grad>. Shift + Right-click with the appropriate food item under the Taming section to initiate breeding. Should you breed one male dragon that you own with a female dragon that someone else owns, or otherwise, the baby will default to following the female.
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Go to the next page to view their abilities.</grad>
## <grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Keys - Abilities</grad>
&2Left-click&r: Bite.
&2X&r to switch to Horn Gore.
&2Holding G&r: Shoots out water breath. While shooting, press &2X&r to switch to poison.
&2G&r - Clawing: Swipes at your enemies.
[gap=5]
&2R&r - Roar: Shoots out venomous spines, stunning your enemy while poisoning them.
&2Holding R&r - Charge up a Poison Ball and shoot upon release.

## <grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>Hunger</grad>
A dragon needs to eat to keep its stats up, and if it's low on hunger it will become weak and slow.
[gap=5]
[gap=5]
Using a dragon in any way, such as Flying, Walking, using skills, etc. will drain their hunger.
If your dragon's hunger is &660-31&r, stats will be lowered. If it's below &630&r, damage output will be capped to &f&l<grad from=#FFFFFF to=#FFFFFF><shake a=0.8 f=0.8>25%%</shake></grad>&r!
<grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>"So what do I feed them?!"</grad> - You may ask.
Fish! Yeah, raw fish. These things are like cats, just not as cute..
A questline without a guide?
&lALL&r dragons eat fish, though some of them enjoy other food as well. That's something you gotta discover yourself!

[gap=5]

[gap=5]
A dragon needs to be really happy, so it'll be down to travel with you, or else..
[gap=0]
[gap=5]
Dragons will get more and more sad by 2 points every 8 minutes.
[gap=0]
If a dragon receives damage, they'll lose
[gap=0]
2 points as well.
If a dragon's happiness is &660-31&r, they'll be really slow. If it's below &630&r, their speed will be limited to &f&l<grad from=#FFFFFF to=#FFFFFF><shake a=1 f=0.9>50%%</shake></grad>&r.
And.. they won't let you ride them. Yup.
To straighten up their mood, you have to either &6feed them&r or use a &eBrush&r!
A normal brush increases happiness by &f1&r, a &egolden&r one increases it by &f3&r, every &f2&r successful right-clicks.
I can't let that slide..
[gap=5]
[gap=5]

The Chest is most likely going to be the first way that you store your items more permanantly. Crafted from Logs or Planks, the Chest can hold 27 stacks of items.
Combining two chests into a double chest by placing them beside each other nets you 54 total stacks of item storage - perfect for storing your early game spoils.
Right-click with a packing tape to a chest or barrel, then break it!
The Barrel Functions similarly to the Chest, but it does not require an empty space above it to be opened.
The only downside is that you cannot combine the inventory of two Barrels.
The Barrel holds 27 stacks of items.
This Quest serves to remind you that you do not have to break your existing chests or barrels to upgrade them!
Instead, craft the proper storage upgrade and right click to upgrade your existing storage!

The wooden drawers are the main storage block in Storage Drawers, and they come in a variety of configurations and styles.
You can pick from any of the Vanilla plank colors when crafting to get a matching block.
Drawers come in six configurations, trading capacity and compactness at different points:

Drawer Controllers are the second special block in the mod. They will search for any connected drawer within 50 blocks by default (configurable), and then expose all of those drawers as a large combined inventory. This lets you hook up a single pipe, tube, duct, spark, storage bus, etc. and interact with the entire bank of drawers.

Compacting drawers are a special stone-colored drawer that auto-convert compressible items. Instead of manually crafting and uncrafting between gold blocks, ingots, and nuggets, put them in a compacting drawer. The drawer will internally maintain a pool of the material, and let you insert or remove the material in any of the shown forms.
The compacting behavior applies to any item with a compressed recipe, including redstone, glowstone, wheat, and many others.
Collects items in-world from a big distance
Increases storage by 8 times base value

When a turn table rotates the block (providing it's connected to the right axis) it will start deploying or retracting the items it's holding in its inventory, depending if it's getting rotated clock-wise or counter clock-wise.
When rotated it will also rotate all the pulleys adjacent to it that are facing the same axis.

Pulleys can be chained together. This means that all chains and ropes being pulled/pushed by a pulley will have the chance to be accepted by a receiving pulley. When this happens they will also have the chance to be placed horizontally which is usually not possible. This effect has a 6 blocks range. Keep in mind that pulleys will always prioritize the down direction or a direction where a rope /chain is already present when unwinding.
Just like ropes, pulling up or down chains and ropes with a pulley will also bring up and down the last block attached to the chain.
Pairs well with Turn Table.
Can also be rotated by using a crank.

A metallic block is defined as block made of Iron or Copper Ingots or made of things made of Iron or Copper Ingots.
When powered, the front (red) side of the Magnet will push blocks forward like a piston, whereas the back side will pull blocks towards it.
When multiple Magnets are exerting force upon the same block, the one with the biggest force wins. A magnet's force upon a block is the redstone level applied to it, minus the distance.
When a Hopper is moved by a magnet, it'll drop one of its items directly in front of it. If this item is a seed, it'll plant it either directly in front or one block down.
When a Stonecutter is moved by a magnet, it'll break any block above it. Very hard blocks such as Obsidian can't be broken like this.

&aTips:&r
Comparators can read how many items are in a pipe.
A pipe with 15 or more items inside at once will break.
Copper Pipes are rudimentary and don't include any filtering or pathing for items. The way items flow in pipes is based in a small set of rules:
&6&oApply the following rules in order&r&r
1. Items will move down if possible.
2. Items will move forward if possible.
3. At an intersection, items will randomly flow left or right.
4. Items will not go upwards unless that's the only possibility.
5. Items attempting to enter a full container will bounce back (Items bouncing back from pipes remember which direction they came into the pipe, and will avoid that direction).
6. Items will not flow into disabled (powered) pipes.
{image:fzzy_config:textures/gui/sprites/widget/popup/divider.png width:200 height:15 align:center}

ftbquests.chapter.tameable_beast.quest22: The &dDeed of Ownership&r is an item that can be purchased from the &cAnimal Tamer&r villager.
By &6right clicking&r on a tamed pet that you are the owner of, you bind it to the deed.

If you give the deed to any other player, and they use it on the bounded animal, then the ownership of the pet will pass onto them, making it effectively as if they were the ones who tamed the animal.

ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest74: Picks up things from the nearby area.
99.9%% chance to not scare your pets
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest75: Smashes things into more broken things.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest76: Rapidly freezes items and fluids, can use a forming cast with fluids to determine the output.
Increases processing speed at the cost of higher RF usage.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest78: Restricts quantity and flow of items.
Fills containers with fluids. This is required to consume certain beverages.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest79: Grows and reforms crystalline structures. Requires a fluid and at least one item.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest7B: Destroys anything placed inside into it. (fluids included)
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest7F: Destroys excess in case of buildup.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.quest98: Multipurpose table for augmenting, recharging, and refilling.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.questA4: In the beginning was the Machine Frame, and the Machine Frame was with Thermal Series, and the Machine Frame was Thermal Series.
ftbquests.chapter.thermal_series.title: <shadow><grad from=#EC0909 to=#FCF241>Thermal Series</grad></shadow>

ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest1: <shadow><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.3>Axes can be used in dispensers to strip or scrape blocks.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.6>Stripping or scraping blocks by dispenser</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest2: <shadow><grad from=#8B4513 to=#CD853F f=0.25>Anything can be done from your inventory now!</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#C0C0C0 to=#5A5A5A f=0.3>Chains connect blocks together when a piston moves them.</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>You can gain </grad><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>additional wood</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> by placing </grad><grad from=#8B4513 to=#CD853F f=0.8>wooden furniture</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> on top of a </grad><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.8>chopping board</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> and chopping it with an </grad><grad from=#5FA9C0 to=#D1E8EE f=0.8>axe</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#8B4513 to=#CD853F f=0.6>More Wood With Careful Chopping</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>Linggango</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> features numerous </grad><grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>hidden mechanics</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> and </grad><grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>mechanics</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> that you might easily overlook during normal </grad><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.8>gameplay</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>.</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>This </grad><grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.5>questline</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> is dedicated to teaching you essential </grad><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>Quality of Life</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> (</grad><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>QoL</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>) tips to make your overall experience </grad><wave a=0.3 f=0.5 w=0.5><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>smoother</grad></wave><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFA040 to=#FFD080 f=0.5>Quality of Life Tricks</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFD700 to=#5A4200 f=0.3>Pistons are allowed to move Tile Entities (blocks with extra data attached, like Chests or Furnaces).</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFD700 to=#5A4200 f=0.6>Pistons Move Tile Entities</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest3: Breaking grass or crops with a &aHoe&r will now break them in a 3x3 area.
A &aDiamond Hoe&r will break a 5x5 area instead.
You can add a &3Fortune enchantement&r to a &aHoe&r to get extra drops from grass and crops.
<shadow><grad from=#4FD5BD to=#9EF9E8 f=0.25>Learn how to use a hoe better.</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest4: <shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0>When you do so, the </grad><grad from=#4EF44E to=#BBFF99 f=0.5>Vine</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0> will no longer be able to grow.</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#4EF44E to=#BBFF99 f=0.3>The tip of a vine can now be </grad><grad from=#B2FFD1 to=#D4FFEA f=0.3>right-clicked</grad><grad from=#4EF44E to=#BBFF99 f=0.25> with Shears.</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest5: <shadow><grad from=#A3E1BD to=#F8FAE6 f=0.25>Stores enchantments but better</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#32CD32 to=#006400 f=0.3>Stores enchantment books, sadly, not much people use it..</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>Essences has a lot of uses in this pack, but this one is the </grad><grad from=#FF69B4 to=#FFD1E8 f=0.8>cutest one :D!</grad></shadow>
<shadow><wave a=0.3 f=0.5 w=0.5><grad from=#FF69B4 to=#FFD1E8 f=0.8>They're gaming now.</grad></wave></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>If you use the </grad><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>Great Essence</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> on a </grad><grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.8>Cat</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> or </grad><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#E8E8E8 f=0.8>Wolf</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> they will transform into a more.. </grad><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.8>useful creature</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>After using the great essence on them:</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>After dropping some items to them:</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.3>Dog looks so goofy</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest7: <shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>Speed up smelting with </grad><grad from=#8B4513 to=#CD853F f=0.8>Bellows</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>!</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>-Place next to a </grad><grad from=#A0A0A0 to=#E8E8E8 f=0.8>furnace</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> (not on top)</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>-Activate with </grad><grad from=#FF4D4D to=#FFD9D9 f=0.8>redstone</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> to boost </grad><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>smelting speed</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#8B4513 f=0.3>Speed up smelting with furnaces.</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>Placed </grad><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.8>books</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> can give </grad><grad from=#FFA333 to=#FCDFB8 f=0.8>enchanting power</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> to an </grad><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.8>Enchanting Table</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>. The power given depends on the number and type of </grad><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.8>books</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> used.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#5FA9C0 to=#D1E8EE f=0.3>Enchanted \&\ normal books can be placed and thus have a block form.</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>With </grad><grad from=#FFABAB to=#D1C4FF f=0.8>enchantment infuser</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3>, you can choose the </grad><grad from=#5FA9C0 to=#D1E8EE f=0.8>specific enchantment</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C7C7C7 f=0.3> you want on your tool.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFD700 to=#FFFF99 f=0.8>Advanced version comes with repairing.</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFABAB to=#D1C4FF f=0.25>No more relying on luck!</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.quest8: <shadow><grad from=#4EF44E to=#BBFF99 f=0.5>Creeper</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0> exploded and destroyed your </grad><grad from=#4EF44E to=#BBFF99 f=0.5>terrain</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0>?</grad></shadow>

<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0>Just throw the </grad><grad from=#4EF44E to=#8B4513 f=0.5>hole filler</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0> into a hole and it will start </grad><shake a=0.75 f=0.75><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0>expanding like crazy</grad></shake><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0>!</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0>There's other variations check </grad><grad from=#FFA040 to=#FFD080 f=0.5>JEI</grad><grad from=#FFFFFF to=#C0C0C0> to see</grad></shadow>
<shadow><grad from=#4EF44E to=#8B4513 f=0.15>This is really handy.</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.questC: <shadow><grad from=#FF8888 to=#FFD9D9 f=0.25>In the Nether, the compass points to the portal through which you entered the Nether.</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.questD: Very useful if you prefer a smaller dragon.
<shadow><grad from=#42FF42 to=#9400D3 f=0.3>Stops ageing when fed to young animals.</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.questE: <shadow><grad from=#42FF42 to=#BAFFBA f=0.3>Feeding rotten flesh to a tamed skeleton horse will slowly convert it into a zombie horse.</grad></shadow>
ftbquests.chapter.tips__additions.title: <shadow a=0.4><grad from=#B4654B to=#C4C6CA>Tips \& Tricks</grad></shadow>

ftbquests.chapter.toms_simple_storage.qu: Think of wires all connecting from several places into a single point.
What if you have 2 storages in different locations? Use this!
If you know how Create's item vault and filters work, this is similar.
Basically a advanced hopper with filter
Extension cables, but instead of electricity, it is essentially an item cable.
Have a look at your entire inventory like a computer looking at all their files in one place and access them.
Mainly placed on an &6inventory connector block&r.
No more looking for 50 chests to find one item
You can craft while you're away from your main base, but remember that it is quite costly for such a benefit.

ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest164AAA: Elytra allows you to float in the sky!
With fireworks and some artifacts you can also get a huge speed boost.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest2674AD: Sturdy, has 3 seats, and a bunch of weapon options
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest3A643C: Fast, one seat, medium difficulty. Make sure you have somewhere flat to take off and land on!
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest41540B: Powered with redstone, faster than a normal rail but can't turn or slope
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest479F5E: Slower, but with two seats. Quite easy to maneuver, with acceptable speed thanks to the engine in the back.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest51AEDA: Human-powered primitive dual seater. Trusty and easy to maneuver, yet not the fastest. Needs a good initial push to get the rotor spinning.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest55FAE2: An advanced version of the airship, it can carry a large number of items.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest62CC0A: Easy to make, not that good.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest66DA4E: A masterwork of engineering! Four propellers strapped on a bunch of bamboos. It's quite handy for building.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest70E388: A normal rail with faster maximum speed
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.quest7652B7: Discover new and efficient methods for traversing both land and sea.
In addition to upgraded boats and unique mounts, the pack includes functional wagons to help transport large quantities of items over long distances.
Check through JEI to view all available transport options and their crafting recipes.
ftbquests.chapter.travelling.title: <shadow a=0.4><grad from=#6F6D8B to=#343434>Traveling</grad></shadow>

I mean i probably don't even need to explain it since everyone knows it.
Well, all i can say is the dimension itself is very beautiful and unique!
ftbquests.chapter.twilight_forest.quest6: &bLich&r was a person back in the time, though he experimented with undead so much that he died.
aand became an undead. Now he controls them.
&aNaga&r is a overdevelopped snake grown in the twilight forest's weird nature.
It will the first boss we will be fighting so get ready!
ftbquests.chapter.twilight_forest.quest7: &bHydra&r is a godzilla wannabe dragon that can unleash some spicy stuff.

ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest1: damage scales infinitely with gambling accessories.
excels vs bosses due to high base damage.
feels like vanilla sword but pure power.
&69.&r Duskfire Blade — Infinite flame debuff crushes bosses.
&610.r Ancient Sage Bow — Reliable ranged option for dragon/wither fights.
&61.&r Midas Touch — By this point, scaling accessories make it absurdly good.
&62.&r Real Knife ×2 — When procced properly, unmatched burst; slightly inconsistent.
&63.&r Blighted Cleaver ×2 — Versatile range and DoT stacking.
&64.&r Exalted Oathblade ×2 — Similar to Cleaver, slightly less damage.
&65.&r Aquaflora — Early weapon that stays viable; still fast and fun.
&66.&r The Judge — Skill-based powerhouse when used right.
&67.&r Nightfall — Huge absorption buff = mega tank.
&68.&r Eye of the Sun — Strong DPS and AoE, easy pick-up-and-play.
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest2: Dual wield for major mob-melting potential.
infinite flame debuff, great for bosses.
AoE burn, pierces mobs, strong choice.
excellent mobility and AoE. // Can be used in mid-game.
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest3: can push you back, odd utility.
can be infused with spells, great if found early.
&41.&r Nightmare Sword — Do we even need to explain?
&42.&r Gambler’s Blade — Insane scaling with gambling builds; rival to Nightmare.
&43.&r Starlight — Magic damage, armor piercing, godly with lifesteal.
&44.&r Gael’s Greatsword — Consistent, ranged, excellent DPS + debuffs.
&45.&r Violence — Straightforward DPS monster, reliable handling.
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest4: solid AoE and range, strong starter.
&a1.&r Ancient Sage Bow — Insanely high per-shot damage; can carry through early bosses.
&a2.&r Diamond Halberd — Consistent DPS, great range, easy to use.
&a3.&r Diamond Sai ×2 — High DPS ramp-up, limited reach.
&a4.&r Diamond Longsword ×2 — Reliable damage, good AoE and spacing.
&a5.&r Diamond Katana — Balanced pick; solid in any encounter.
long reach and double DoT stacking.
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest5: can act as bow, versatile tool.
&e1.&r Midas Touch — With Fateful Coin, ridiculous scaling and versatility.
&e2.&r Aquaflora — Fast, mobile, and fun; fits any playstyle.
&e3.&r Wickpiercer — Off-hand this with Midas Touch for destruction.
&e4.&r Keres — Charged attack deals huge burst; health cost is manageable.
&e5.&r Ancient Sage Bow — Still a strong option against Wither or Dragon.
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest6: powerful AoE and stable high DPS.
meh, skip. (BONG sound effect is peak)
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest7: (4,200 w/ Fateful Coin); gambler’s dream combo.
travel weapon, doubles any damage taken.
ftbquests.chapter.weapon_tierlist.quest8: &e1.&r Midas Touch — With Fateful Coin, ridiculous scaling and versatility.
&e2.&r Aquaflora — Fast, mobile, and fun; fits any playstyle.
&e3.&r Wickpiercer — Off-hand this with Midas Touch for destruction.
&e4.&r Keres — Charged attack deals huge burst; health cost is manageable.
&e5.&r Ancient Sage Bow — Still a strong option against Wither or Dragon.
